question_text: A ball is thrown horizontally with speed 15 m/s from the top of a 45 m cliff. Find the time of flight, horizontal distance covered, and speed just before hitting the ground.
A stone is projected from level ground with speed 20 m/s at an angle of 30 degrees above the horizontal. Find its time of flight, maximum height, and horizontal range.
A projectile is launched from ground level with speed 40 m/s. Find the launch angle for maximum range and the value of that maximum range.
A projectile is fired with speed 50 m/s and lands 216.5 m away on the same horizontal level. Find the possible angles of projection.

A ball is projected at 25 m/s making an angle of 37 degrees with the horizontal. Use sin 37 degrees = 0.6 and cos 37 degrees = 0.8. Find the horizontal and vertical components of its initial velocity.

A ball is thrown from the top of a 60 m building with speed 20 m/s at 30 degrees above the horizontal. Find the time taken to reach the ground and the horizontal distance from the base of the building.
A ball is thrown from a height of 80 m with speed 25 m/s at 37 degrees below the horizontal. Use sin 37 degrees = 0.6 and cos 37 degrees = 0.8. Find the time of flight and horizontal range.
A projectile is launched with speed 30 m/s at an angle of 45 degrees. A vertical wall is 45 m from the point of projection. Find the height of the projectile when it reaches the wall.
A projectile is fired at 45 degrees and lands 80 m away on level ground. Find the required initial speed.
The vertical component of the initial velocity of a projectile is 30 m/s. Find the two times at which it is at a height of 40 m above the point of projection.
A projectile is fired with speed 20 m/s at 60 degrees above the horizontal. Find its horizontal and vertical coordinates after 1 second.
A projectile is launched with speed 25 m/s and must hit a target 50 m away on the same horizontal level. Find the possible angles of projection.
Two projectiles are launched with the same speed of 40 m/s, one at 30 degrees and the other at 60 degrees. Compare their time of flight, maximum height, and range.

A monkey hangs from a branch 45 m above the ground. A hunter aims directly at the monkey and fires a projectile with speed 30 m/s. At the same instant, the monkey lets go. Determine whether the projectile hits the monkey before the monkey reaches the ground.
A projectile is fired with speed 20 m/s at 60 degrees to the horizontal up an inclined plane of angle 30 degrees. Find the range measured along the incline.
A football is kicked from ground level with speed 18 m/s at an angle of 35 degrees. Find the time it stays in the air and the distance from the kicker where it lands.
A ball is launched from a balcony 25 m above the ground with horizontal speed 12 m/s. Find how long it remains in air and how far from the building it lands.
A stone is thrown upward at 20 m/s from the edge of a cliff 30 m high. It has no horizontal velocity. Find the total time before it reaches the ground.
A projectile is launched from ground with speed 30 m/s at 53 degrees. Use sin 53 degrees = 0.8 and cos 53 degrees = 0.6. Find its maximum height and range.
A ball is thrown horizontally from a height of 20 m and lands 30 m away from the foot of the tower. Find the initial horizontal speed.
A projectile is fired from ground level and takes 6 seconds to return to the ground. If its horizontal range is 180 m, find the horizontal and vertical components of initial velocity.
A ball is projected at 40 m/s at an angle theta. Its time of flight is 4 seconds. Find theta and the horizontal range.
A projectile has a maximum height of 45 m and a time of flight of 6 seconds. Find its vertical component of initial velocity and verify whether the two quantities are consistent.
A projectile is launched at 30 degrees from level ground. Its range is 100 m. Find the initial speed needed.
A ball is thrown at 10 m/s horizontally from a moving cart that is 5 m above the ground. Find its time of flight and the horizontal distance covered before impact.
A stone is projected from ground level with speed 50 m/s at 37 degrees. Use sin 37 degrees = 0.6 and cos 37 degrees = 0.8. Find the position and velocity of the stone after 3 seconds.
A projectile is fired from level ground with initial speed 28 m/s at an unknown angle. Its maximum height is 19.6 m. Find the angle of projection and the range.
A ball is thrown from a 100 m tall tower with speed 20 m/s horizontally. Find its impact speed and the angle made by the velocity with the horizontal just before impact.
A projectile is launched with speed 60 m/s at 30 degrees. Find the time after launch when it reaches half of its maximum height on the way up and on the way down.
A projectile is fired with speed 35 m/s at 45 degrees. Find the height of the projectile when it has covered half of its horizontal range.
A ball is thrown from the ground and passes through a point 20 m high after 2 seconds. If its horizontal velocity is 15 m/s, find the initial vertical velocity and its launch speed.
A projectile is launched at 60 degrees with speed 40 m/s. Find the time at which its velocity makes an angle of 30 degrees with the horizontal while moving upward.
A projectile is fired horizontally from the top of a 125 m high cliff. It lands 100 m from the base. Find the initial speed and the velocity just before impact.
A stone is thrown from ground level with speed 20 m/s at 45 degrees. Find the equation of its trajectory in terms of x and y.
A ball is projected from the top of a building with speed 30 m/s at 60 degrees above the horizontal. The building is 80 m high. Find where the ball lands relative to the base of the building.
A projectile is fired from ground level at 45 degrees. It reaches a maximum height of 20 m. Find its initial speed and horizontal range.
A cricket ball is hit at 25 m/s at 40 degrees above the horizontal. A fielder is standing 50 m away. Find the height of the ball when it reaches the fielder.
A projectile is launched with speed 32 m/s at 30 degrees. Find the time at which it reaches a horizontal distance of 48 m and then find its height at that point.
A ball is thrown upward from a 20 m high platform at an angle of 45 degrees with speed 20 m/s. Find the total time of flight and horizontal distance travelled.

A projectile is launched at 37 degrees with a horizontal component of velocity 24 m/s. Use sin 37 degrees = 0.6 and cos 37 degrees = 0.8. Find its initial speed, vertical component, time of flight, and range.
A ball rolls off a table of height 1.25 m and lands 2 m from the base of the table. Find the horizontal speed with which it left the table.
A projectile is fired at 60 degrees and returns to the same level after 8 seconds. Find the initial speed and the range.
A ball is projected from level ground with speed 30 m/s. It reaches a maximum height of 11.25 m. Find the angle of projection and the horizontal range.
A projectile is launched from the ground and its velocity at the highest point is 20 m/s. Its total time of flight is 6 seconds. Find its initial speed and range.
A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a tower. It strikes the ground after 4 seconds at a horizontal distance of 60 m. Find the height of the tower and the initial speed.
A projectile is fired at 30 degrees with speed 40 m/s. Find the time after launch when its height is 15 m.
A ball is thrown at 50 m/s at 53 degrees above the horizontal. Use sin 53 degrees = 0.8 and cos 53 degrees = 0.6. Find the time when the vertical velocity becomes zero and the height at that instant.
A projectile is fired from the ground at speed 25 m/s. It lands on a platform 10 m high and 40 m away. Find the possible angles of projection.
A projectile is launched from a cliff 50 m high at speed 20 m/s and 30 degrees below the horizontal. Find the time to hit the ground and the range from the cliff base.
A ball is thrown from ground level at 20 m/s and 60 degrees. Find its displacement from the starting point after 2 seconds.
A projectile is launched with speed 45 m/s at 30 degrees. Find the height and vertical velocity when it has travelled 90 m horizontally.
A projectile is fired so that its horizontal range is four times its maximum height. Find the angle of projection.
A ball is launched from ground level and has equal maximum height and horizontal range numerically. Find the projection angle.
A projectile is launched from ground level at an angle theta with speed u. Derive the condition on theta for maximum horizontal range on level ground.
A projectile is fired with speed 30 m/s at an angle of 75 degrees. Find the time of flight, maximum height, and range.

A stone is projected from the top of a 40 m tower with speed 25 m/s at 37 degrees above horizontal. Use sin 37 degrees = 0.6 and cos 37 degrees = 0.8. Find the total time of flight and horizontal distance covered.

A projectile is fired from the ground. At t = 2 seconds, its coordinates are x = 30 m and y = 20 m. Find its initial horizontal velocity, initial vertical velocity, initial speed, and angle of projection.
A ball is thrown horizontally from a height h with speed 10 m/s and lands 40 m away. Find h.
A projectile is fired at 45 degrees. If the maximum height reached is 25 m, find the time of flight and the range.

A projectile is launched from a point on the ground and just clears two walls of equal height 10 m located at horizontal distances 10 m and 30 m from the point of projection. Find the angle of projection and initial speed.
